commands.review_summary:
The PR adds shared OpenTelemetry metric advice for diagnostics-otel millisecond duration histograms through 600000 ms and asserts harness histogram boundaries in the service test.

PR surface: Source +23, Tests +4. Total +27 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main creates the affected millisecond histograms without explicit bucket advice, while OpenTelemetry sdk-metrics defaults finite histogram buckets through 10000. I did not run a live collector reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The protected maintainer label, missing real behavior proof, and competing open bucket-boundary PR require maintainer review rather than a ClawSweeper repair job.

target.title: fix(otel): extend duration histogram buckets
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

OpenTelemetry's default millisecond histogram has no finite bucket above 10 seconds. Percentile queries therefore report exactly 10 seconds when long-running harness or agent operations fall into the final unbounded bucket, hiding the real p95.

## Why This Change Was Made

Apply explicit duration boundaries through 10 minutes to all millisecond OTEL histograms. The boundaries match the existing Prometheus diagnostics exporter, keeping percentile behavior consistent across both telemetry paths.

## User Impact

Operators can see useful p50 and p95 values for long-running harness, model, tool, message, queue, and other duration metrics instead of a false 10-second plateau. New samples are affected; previously aggregated samples cannot be recovered.

## Evidence

- Added a regression assertion that the harness duration histogram includes a 30-second bucket and ends at 10 minutes.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/diagnostics-otel/src/service.test.ts` — 95 tests passed.
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check extensions/diagnostics-otel/src/service.ts extensions/diagnostics-otel/src/service.test.ts` — passed.
- The normal `pnpm test` wrapper could not start tests because dependency reconciliation was blocked by Socket Firewall with HTTP 403 for `acpx@0.11.2`; no policy bypass was attempted.
